hero_desc: A day of unity and celebration for the African diaspora.
next_event_desc: Join us for the first BJuneteenth community meet-up — a gathering bringing together members of the Black diaspora as we begin preparing for Juneteenth Barcelona 2026. Connection, collaboration, and building momentum together. DJ Chanti takes over at 22:00.
next_event_location: 📍 The Station Bar, Carrer de Sant Bertran 14, Ciutat Vella
next_event_series_note: Part of a monthly series on the 19th of each month.
intro_body:
Juneteenth commemorates June 19, 1865 — the day enslaved people in Texas learned they were free, two and a half years after the Emancipation Proclamation.

In Barcelona, we honor this history, celebrate Black culture, and build bridges across the African diaspora living in and visiting the city.
intro_quote: "A city meaningfully connected to the African diaspora — whose stories deserve to be told."
wt_body: Trace the visible and invisible footprints of Black diasporic presence through Barcelona — from medieval accounts of African residents, to monuments funded by colonial profits, to the hubs of knowledge open today. A 2–3 hour self-guided walk through the city's hidden history.
